title: Performance Guidelines for Component Logic and Implementation
breadcrumbs: Development Considerations for Performance and Scalability
Performance Guidelines for Component Logic and Implementation
text: Performance Guidelines for Component Logic and Implementation Even with an optimized application model, there are still design and implementation decisions that can affect performance, such as your choice of components, data retrieval implementation, component data structure and indexing decisions. Uniface enables you to choose the best-of-breed components for implementing specific functionality and to integrate components from different tool sets into a single, cohesive application architecture. The choice of components, implementation technology, and component logic can affect application performance. Component logic largely defines what resources are accessed and how they are accessed. To implement component logic efficiently, consider the following: Keep the design as simple as possible. If the required functionality is very complicated, it is better to build the functionality into a number of simple components than into one complex component. Uniface supports complex functionality, but simplicity ensures easy implementation and maintenance. Use a single scan through the data set rather than repetitive loops when retrieving the same data. This makes better use of the available computing resources. Limit the scope of the component with respect to the data that it accesses. You can do this using filter forms, which define the data to be accessed before the actual processing is performed. Use asynchronous and stateless requests whenever possible. This can reduce contention problems and enhance server sharing. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
